plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and exit positions below stop.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability.
Buy ANET and NOW at 5% each, adding high-quality, strong scanner setups.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and reduced volatility. Expect lower drawdowns but also slower growth, with a strong emphasis on protecting against market downturns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration and rebalance broad market exposure.
Sell SOXL entirely to remove a position below its stop.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong performer.
Buy ANET at 5% to add a high-quality name from the scanner.
Buy TECL at 3% to add a small, leveraged tech exposure from a strong scanner setup.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, aiming for growth with a focus on risk management. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and a small, controlled amount of leverage, expecting moderate drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and reallocate capital.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate to stronger setups.
Increase CRWD to 25% to maximize exposure to a top performer.
Buy TECL at 10% to add significant leveraged tech exposure from a strong scanner setup.
Buy ARM at 10% and AVGO at 5% to add high-scoring semiconductor names.
This plan uses leverage and concentrates in high-scoring setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. Be aware that this approach significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown potential, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay and rebalancing, leading to potentially rapid losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration and reduce broad market volatility.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 30% for enhanced income and stability.
Buy GLD at 5% to add a defensive asset for portfolio stability.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and reliable income.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable and the NAV can still decline in market selloffs; this is not a savings account and carries market risk.
